testCases.title: Send valid tracking code → receive full status reply
testCases.preconditions: Real CE Express tracking code available. Bot state is clean.
steps: Open Telegram and start a chat with the bot
Send a valid CE Express tracking code as plain text (e.g. CEX123456789)
testCases.expectedResult: Bot replies with: shipment status label, full event history with timestamps, package weight, masked destination address. No error message shown.
testCases.notes: Note the exact response format for future regression checks.

testCases.title: Send invalid tracking code → error message shown
steps: Open Telegram and start a chat with the bot
Send a fake tracking code: INVALID000000
testCases.expectedResult: Bot replies with a user-friendly error message indicating the code was not found. Bot does NOT crash or go silent.
testCases.notes: Message should be human-readable, not a raw API error.

testCases.title: Out for Delivery status includes courier name and phone
testCases.preconditions: A tracking code with status Out for Delivery (code 40) is available.
steps: Send the tracking code with status 40 to the bot
testCases.expectedResult: Reply includes courier name and courier phone number in addition to standard tracking info.
testCases.notes: Per PRD 6.1 — courier info only shown for status 40.

testCases.title: Destination address is masked in reply
testCases.preconditions: Any valid tracking code with a destination address.
steps: Send a valid tracking code to the bot
Inspect the destination address in the reply
testCases.expectedResult: Address is partially masked — shows city/province but not full street address.

testCases.title: Re-check same code within 5 min → rate limited
testCases.preconditions: A valid tracking code that was just checked (within last 5 minutes).
steps: Send a valid tracking code to the bot
Send the same tracking code again
testCases.expectedResult: Bot replies with a rate limit message. Does NOT re-fetch. Indicates when the user can check again.

testCases.title: Re-check same code after 5 min → succeeds
testCases.preconditions: A valid tracking code that was checked more than 5 minutes ago.
testCases.expectedResult: Bot fetches and returns fresh tracking data. No rate limit message.

testCases.title: Adding 10 tracking codes all succeed
testCases.preconditions: Clean state — no existing tracked codes. 10 different valid codes ready.
steps: Send 10 different tracking codes one by one
After each, confirm the bot replies normally
testCases.expectedResult: All 10 codes accepted. Bot replies with tracking info for each. No errors.

testCases.title: Adding 11th tracking code is rejected
testCases.preconditions: User already has 10 tracked codes (run TC-TRACK-007 first).
steps: Send an 11th new tracking code to the bot
testCases.expectedResult: Bot replies with error explaining the maximum limit is reached. 11th code is NOT added. Existing 10 codes unaffected.
